symptoms.symptom_name: Severe unremitting bursting headache over 6 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cerebral oedema from breakthrough hyperperfusion produces severe progressive headache.
Severe headache mandates exclusion of intracranial haemorrhage.
Patient-obtainable home reading; extreme BP plus symptoms defines a hypertensive emergency. Admissible in telehealth but unverified.
Retinal and occipital involvement from severe hypertension causes visual field disturbance.
Visual aura also occurs in migraine, but the surrounding features exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Carer-reported new confusion, lethargy and repetitive questioning

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Altered mental status is the defining cerebral end-organ feature that makes this an emergency.
New neurology requires imaging to exclude stroke.
Abrupt withdrawal of antihypertensives is a recognised precipitant of a rebound hypertensive crisis.
SAH classically presents as instantaneous maximal-onset headache; a gradual build makes it less likely as the primary cause but does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured BP 225/135 mmHg, manual, both arms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician measurement (both arms also screens for dissection); the AI Doctor infers the emergency from the patient-offered home reading, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suspected papilloedema / flame haemorrhages / cotton-wool spots on fundoscopy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Grade III/IV hypertensive retinopathy on fundoscopy confirms malignant/accelerated hypertension — a clinician-only finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Confusion with GCS 14, no focal neurological deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Global depression of consciousness without focal signs favours encephalopathy over territorial stroke — a formal neuro exam, sealed.
Acute kidney injury is renal end-organ damage confirming an emergency — a hospital blood test, never available in telehealth.
Cardiac end-organ strain/ischaemia supports an emergency — a clinician investigation, sealed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Distinguish hypertensive encephalopathy from acute ischaemic stroke and intracranial/subarachnoid haemorrhage — done in hospital after emergency transport.
Detect acute kidney injury as renal end-organ damage.
Screen for acute cardiac ischaemia / LV strain from the pressure load.
Assess for papilloedema and hypertensive retinopathy confirming malignant hypertension — clinician-performed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Oral antihypertensive challenge at home to titrate the number down
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Home oral lowering cannot be controlled and risks cerebral/renal/coronary hypoperfusion — the AI Doctor must NOT do this; controlled IV titration in hospital is required.
